housing_laws.summary: Minnesota has strong tenant protections including cold weather rule, statutory right to privacy, and limits on landlord entry.

notice_requirements.termination_lease_end: No notice required if lease specifies end date
notice_requirements.nonpayment: 14 days to pay or vacate (No notice for manufactured home parks)
notice_requirements.notes: Written notice required. Email/text only valid if tenant agreed in writing.

security_deposit.notes: Landlord must provide written statement of reasons for withholding any deposit.

rent_control.local_programs: Minneapolis has some affordable housing programs but no broad rent control
rent_control.notes: State law preempts local rent control. Tenant can challenge unreasonable rent increases in court under 'unconscionable' standard.

tenant_remedies.notes: Tenant must give 14 days notice for repairs. Amount deducted cannot exceed $500 or one month's rent.

repairs.cold_weather_rule: Nov 1 - Apr 15: Utility shutoffs prohibited for non-payment if tenant establishes payment plan with utility

landlord_entry.tenant_rights: Tenant can refuse entry without proper notice. Repeated unauthorized entry = landlord harassment.

eviction.timeline: Typically 2-6 weeks from filing to hearing
eviction.expungement: Eviction records can be expunged in certain circumstances

protected_classes: Status with regard to public assistance
discrimination.familial_status: Cannot refuse to rent to families with children

legal_aid.name: HOME Line
legal_aid.services: Free legal advice hotline for MN tenants

legal_aid.name: Legal Aid Service of Northeastern Minnesota (LASNEM)
legal_aid.counties: Aitkin, Carlton, Cook, Itasca, Koochiching, Lake, St. Louis

legal_aid.name: Southern Minnesota Regional Legal Services (SMRLS)
legal_aid.counties: Twin Cities metro and Southern MN

legal_aid.name: Volunteer Lawyers Network
legal_aid.services: Pro bono legal services for low-income Minnesotans

forms_templates.name: Repair Request Letter (MN)
forms_templates.description: MN-specific repair request with statutory citations

special_programs.covid_rental_assistance: RentHelpMN ended but check county programs
special_programs.energy_assistance: Minnesota Energy Assistance Program - helps pay heating bills
special_programs.homestead_credit_refund: Property tax refund for renters with income under $64,000 (2024)

CA.name: California
CA.notes: California housing laws vary by city. Check local rent control ordinances.

NY.name: New York
NY.notes: NYC has rent stabilization and rent control. Upstate NY varies by locality.

TX.name: Texas
TX.notes: Texas has limited tenant protections. Most cities have no rent control.

FL.name: Florida
FL.notes: Florida landlord-tenant law is landlord-friendly. No rent control statewide.

IL.name: Illinois
IL.notes: Cook County and Chicago have additional tenant protections.

PA.name: Pennsylvania
PA.notes: Philadelphia has stronger tenant protections than state law.

WA.name: Washington
WA.notes: Washington recently strengthened tenant protections including just cause eviction.

MA.name: Massachusetts
MA.notes: Strong tenant protections including rent control in some cities.

OR.name: Oregon
OR.notes: Statewide rent control and just cause eviction protections.

CO.name: Colorado
CO.notes: Colorado recently enacted statewide just cause eviction and mobile home protections.

MI.name: Michigan
MI.notes: Detroit has additional local tenant protections.

OH.name: Ohio
OH.notes: Ohio landlord-tenant law varies by city. Cleveland has stronger protections.

NC.name: North Carolina
NC.notes: North Carolina is generally landlord-friendly. No rent control.

GA.name: Georgia
GA.notes: Georgia has weak tenant protections. Local ordinances vary.

AZ.name: Arizona
AZ.notes: Arizona prohibits rent control statewide. Landlord-friendly laws.

VA.name: Virginia
VA.notes: Virginia made significant tenant protection updates in recent years.

NJ.name: New Jersey
NJ.notes: Many NJ cities have rent control. Strong anti-eviction protections.

WI.name: Wisconsin
WI.notes: Wisconsin has statewide housing discrimination protections.

MO.name: Missouri
MO.notes: Missouri is landlord-friendly. Kansas City and St. Louis have some local protections.

AL.name: Alabama
AL.notes: Alabama has limited tenant protections. Check local ordinances.

AK.name: Alaska
AK.notes: Alaska has unique landlord-tenant law due to remote locations.

AR.name: Arkansas
AR.notes: Arkansas is very landlord-friendly. Limited tenant protections.

CT.name: Connecticut
CT.notes: Connecticut has fair housing protections and some rent control.

DE.name: Delaware
DE.notes: Delaware has anti-discrimination laws and some local tenant protections.

HI.name: Hawaii
HI.notes: Hawaii has rent control in some counties and strong eviction protections.

ID.name: Idaho
ID.notes: Idaho is landlord-friendly. No rent control.

IA.name: Iowa
IA.notes: Iowa has fair housing protections but limited rent regulation.

KY.name: Kentucky
KY.notes: Kentucky has fair housing laws and some local protections.

LA.name: Louisiana
LA.notes: Louisiana follows civil law (not common law). Unique landlord-tenant rules.

ME.name: Maine
ME.notes: Maine has strong tenant protections including notice requirements.

MD.name: Maryland
MD.notes: Maryland has statewide eviction protections and some local rent control.

MT.name: Montana
MT.notes: Montana is landlord-friendly. No rent control.

NH.name: New Hampshire
NH.notes: New Hampshire has fair housing laws and some local protections.

NM.name: New Mexico
NM.notes: New Mexico has anti-discrimination and habitability protections.

ND.name: North Dakota
ND.notes: North Dakota is landlord-friendly. Limited tenant protections.

OK.name: Oklahoma
OK.notes: Oklahoma is landlord-friendly. No rent control.

RI.name: Rhode Island
RI.notes: Rhode Island has strong anti-discrimination laws.

SD.name: South Dakota
SD.notes: South Dakota is landlord-friendly. Limited protections.

TN.name: Tennessee
TN.notes: Tennessee is landlord-friendly. No rent control.

UT.name: Utah
UT.notes: Utah is landlord-friendly. Salt Lake City has some protections.

VT.name: Vermont
VT.notes: Vermont has strong tenant protections and some rent control.

WY.name: Wyoming
WY.notes: Wyoming is landlord-friendly. No rent control.
